Sports Injury Analysis

Jack Davis

2026-02-12

It’s all about survival

Preliminaries: Survival analysis concepts

  • Survival just means time to event. In this case the event in question is injury.
  • The Cox Proportional Hazards model is the standard for survival analysis, and it’s used behind the scenes in injurytools.
  • Every unit of exposure (minute played, % of a match played) comes with some per-unit rate of injury called the hazard.
  • Every event has a certain about of loss associated with it. In sports, this can be either days missed or matches missed.
  • Hazard * Expected Loss = Risk

It’s all about survival

So when something is called a risk factor, that means it could increase the chance of an injury, or the time lost associated with an injury.

Many of the books on sports mechanics are about one of these two things.

Sports Biomechanics is big

Sports biomechanics is a huge topic. Here’s a sample of what’s available in the Davis Centre library.

Sports biomechanics is a huge topic. Here’s a sample of what’s available in the Davis Centre library.

Sports injuries as a whole

  • There are two broad categories of sports injuries: acute (all at once) and chronic (building up over time).

  • Acute injuries are handled by avoidance through safety protocols like rules and safety gear.

  • Chronic injuries are handled by mitigation through load management like limiting the number of pitches thrown in baseball, the number of minutes on the court in basketball, and the number of dives taken into the pitch, er, pool.

Types of sports injuries: https://www.niams.nih.gov/health-topics/sports-injuries

Injurytools package

Preamble 2: Custom Functions

convert_profile_url_to_injury = function(profile_url)
{
  # In: https://www.transfermarkt.com/edu-ribeiro/profil/spieler/371250
  # Out: https://www.transfermarkt.com/edu-ribeiro/verletzungen/spieler/371250/plus/1
  
  url = profile_url
  url = str_replace(url, "/profil/", "/verletzungen/")
  url = paste0(url, "/plus/1")
  return(url)
}




get_injuries_player = function(player_url, player_name)
{
  inj_url = convert_profile_url_to_injury(player_url)  
  player_id = str_extract(player_url, "[0-9]+")
  
  
  
  injuries_page <- xml2::read_html(inj_url)
  injuries_page <- injuries_page %>% rvest::html_nodes("#yw1") %>% 
    rvest::html_nodes("tbody") 
  
  if(length(injuries_page) == 0){nInjuries = 0}
  
  if(length(injuries_page) >= 1)
  {
    injuries_page <- injuries_page %>% .[[1]] %>% rvest::html_children()
  }
  
  
  
  nInjuries = length(injuries_page)
  
  season = character(0)
  injury = character(0)
  since = character(0)
  until = character(0)
  days_lost = character(0)
  games_lost = character(0)
  
  injury_acl = character(0)
  injury_type = character(0)
  injury_severity = character(0)
  
  
  if(nInjuries >= 1)
  {
    for(injury_count in 1:nInjuries)
    {
      inj_text = as.character(injuries_page[injury_count])
      inj_bits = unlist(str_extract_all(inj_text, ">.*<"))
      inj_bits = str_replace_all(inj_bits, ">|<", "")
      season = c(season, inj_bits[1])
      injury = c(injury, inj_bits[2])
      since = c(since, inj_bits[3])
      until = c(until, inj_bits[4])
      
      days_lost1 = str_extract(inj_text, "[0-9]+ days<")
      days_lost1 = str_replace(days_lost1, " days<", "")
      days_lost = c(days_lost, days_lost1)
      
      games_lost1 = str_extract(inj_text, "span>[0-9]+")
      games_lost1 = str_replace(games_lost1, "span>", "")
      games_lost = c(games_lost, games_lost1)
      
    }
    
    since = tm_fix_dates2(since)
    until = tm_fix_dates2(until)
    
    injury_acl = rep("NO_ACL", nInjuries)
    injury_acl[str_detect(injury, "Cruciate Ligament")] = "ACL"
    
    injury_type = rep("Unknown", nInjuries)
    injury_type[str_detect(injury, "Ankle|Ligament")] = "Ligament"
    injury_type[str_detect(injury, "Fracture|Foot Injury|Hand Injury|Head Injury|Hip Injury|Pelvis Injury")] = "Bone"
    injury_type[str_detect(injury, "Knock|Concussion")] = "Concussion"
    injury_type[str_detect(injury, "Calf|Groin|Hamstring|Muscl|Pubitis|Thigh")] = "Muscle"
    
    days_lost = as.numeric(days_lost)
    games_lost =as.numeric(games_lost)
    
    injury_severity = rep("Minor", nInjuries)
    injury_severity[days_lost >= 7] = "Moderate"
    injury_severity[days_lost >= 28] = "Severe"
    injury_severity[days_lost >= 90] = "Very_severe"
    
    
  }
  
  player_name = rep(player_name, nInjuries)
  player_id = rep(player_id, nInjuries)
  player_url = rep(player_url, nInjuries)
  inj_url = rep(inj_url, nInjuries)
  from = since
  
  out_df = data.frame(player_name, player_id, season, injury, from, until, days_lost, games_lost, injury, 
                      injury_acl, injury_type, injury_severity)
  
  
  return(out_df)
}




# Had to patch: https://github.com/JaseZiv/worldfootballR/blob/main/R/internals.R

tm_fix_dates2 <- function(dirty_dates) {
  
  fix_date <- function(dirty_date) {
    #print(dirty_date)
    
    dirty3 = unlist(stringr::str_split_fixed(dirty_date, "/", 3))
    dirty_date = paste0(dirty3[3],"-",dirty3[2],"-",dirty3[1])
    dirty_date[dirty_date == "--"] = NA
    clean_date = dirty_date
    return(clean_date)
    
    
    return(clean_date)
  }
  
  clean_dates <- dirty_dates %>% purrr::map_chr(fix_date)
  
  return(clean_dates)
}

Injurytools package

Preamble 3: Data Cleaning

start_year = 2024
br_team_urls <- tm_league_team_urls(country_name = "Brazil", start_year = start_year)
br_team_urls

##  [9] "https://www.transfermarkt.com/corinthians-sao-paulo/startseite/verein/199/saison_id/2024"         
## [10] "https://www.transfermarkt.com/fc-sao-paulo/startseite/verein/585/saison_id/2024"                  
## [11] "https://www.transfermarkt.com/esporte-clube-bahia/startseite/verein/10010/saison_id/2024"         
## [12] "https://www.transfermarkt.com/fluminense-rio-de-janeiro/startseite/verein/2462/saison_id/2024"    
## [13] "https://www.transfermarkt.com/red-bull-bragantino/startseite/verein/8793/saison_id/2024"          
## [14] "https://www.transfermarkt.com/sc-internacional-porto-alegre/startseite/verein/6600/saison_id/2024"
## [15] "https://www.transfermarkt.com/fortaleza-esporte-clube/startseite/verein/10870/saison_id/2024"     
## [16] "https://www.transfermarkt.com/sport-club-do-recife/startseite/verein/8718/saison_id/2024"         
## [17] "https://www.transfermarkt.com/esporte-clube-vitoria/startseite/verein/2125/saison_id/2024"        
## [18] "https://www.transfermarkt.com/ceara-sporting-club/startseite/verein/2029/saison_id/2024"          
## [19] "https://www.transfermarkt.com/esporte-clube-juventude/startseite/verein/10492/saison_id/2024"     
## [20] "https://www.transfermarkt.com/mirassol-futebol-clube-sp-/startseite/verein/3876/saison_id/2024"
for(teamcount in 1:length(br_team_urls))
{
  br_squad = tm_squad_stats(team_url = br_team_urls[teamcount])
  if(teamcount == 1){br_squad_all = br_squad}
  if(teamcount > 1){br_squad_all = rbind(br_squad_all, br_squad)}
  Sys.sleep(5)
  print(teamcount)
}


br_squad_all$player_id = str_extract(br_squad_all$player_url, "[0-9]+$")
br_squad_all$start_year = start_year

br_squad_all = subset(br_squad_all, team_name == "São Paulo Futebol Clube")
br_squad_all = read.csv("Br_Squad_All.csv")

# Get the exposures
raw_br_exposures = ddply(br_squad_all, "player_id", summarize,
                         player_name = player_name[1],
                         year = start_year[1],
                         minutes_played = sum(minutes_played))
# Get the injuries
for(playercount in 1:nrow(br_squad_all))
{
  this_inj_df = get_injuries_player(br_squad_all$player_url[playercount], 
                                    br_squad_all$player_name[playercount])
  if(playercount == 1){br_inj_df = this_inj_df}
  if(playercount > 1){br_inj_df = rbind(br_inj_df, this_inj_df)}
  
  print(br_squad_all$player_url[playercount])
  Sys.sleep(5)
}
br_inj_df = read.csv("Br_Inj_Df.csv")

br_inj_df$from = as.Date(br_inj_df$from)
br_inj_df$until = as.Date(br_inj_df$until)
br_inj_df = subset(br_inj_df, !is.na(from) & !is.na(until))
head(br_inj_df)

Injurytools package

More data prep, as per https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/injurytools/vignettes/prepare-injury-data.html

df_injuries <- prepare_inj(df_injuries0   = br_inj_df,
                           person_id      = "player_name",
                           date_injured   = "from",
                           date_recovered = "until")
head(df_injuries)
##         person_id player_id season        injury date_injured date_recovered
## 1         Jandrei    512344  22/23   Hand injury   2023-05-10     2023-06-30
## 2         Jandrei    512344  22/23      fracture   2022-07-28     2022-08-17
## 3         Jandrei    512344  22/23 Back problems   2022-07-17     2022-07-26
## 4         Jandrei    512344  20/21  Corona virus   2020-11-24     2020-12-10
## 5           Young    894532  24/25   Foot injury   2025-01-17     2025-04-13
## 6 Leandro Mathias    838386  25/26 Knee problems   2025-08-31     2025-11-26
##   days_lost games_lost      injury.1 injury_acl injury_type injury_severity
## 1        52         11   Hand injury     NO_ACL     Unknown          Severe
## 2        21          6      fracture     NO_ACL     Unknown        Moderate
## 3        10          2 Back problems     NO_ACL     Unknown        Moderate
## 4        17          3  Corona virus     NO_ACL     Unknown        Moderate
## 5        87         18   Foot injury     NO_ACL     Unknown          Severe
## 6        88         15 Knee problems     NO_ACL     Unknown          Severe
df_exposures <- prepare_exp(df_exposures0 = raw_br_exposures,
                            person_id     = "player_name",
                            date          = "year",
                            time_expo     = "minutes_played")

head(df_exposures)
##   player_id     person_id date time_expo
## 1     10471  Luiz Gustavo 2024       871
## 2     68097        Rafael 2024      5224
## 3     72441  Rafael Tolói 2024       501
## 4     77100   Lucas Moura 2024      1467
## 5     85314         Oscar 2024      1484
## 6    112988 Cédric Soares 2024      3121
injd <-  prepare_all(data_exposures = df_exposures,
                       data_injuries  = df_injuries,
                       exp_unit = "matches_minutes")
## Warning in check_injfollowup(followup_df, data_injuries): Injury/illness data
## has been cut to the given follow-up period (in exposure data)
head(injd)

Injurytools package

player_list = unique(injd$person_id)
injd1 = subset(injd, person_id %in% player_list[1:10])
injd2 = subset(injd, person_id %in% player_list[11:20])
injd3 = subset(injd, person_id %in% player_list[21:30])
injd4 = subset(injd, person_id %in% player_list[31:40])
injd5 = subset(injd, person_id %in% player_list[41:52])


gg_photo(injd1, 
         title   = "Overview of injuries:\nSao Paolo FC 24/25 sesaon",
         by_date = "2 month", 
         fix     = TRUE) +
  ## plus some lines of ggplot2 code..
  xlab("Follow-up date") + ylab("Players") + labs(caption = "source: transfermarkt.com") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 22),
        axis.text.x.bottom = element_text(size = 13, angle = 20, hjust = 1),
        axis.text.y.left   = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.title.x       = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold", vjust = -1),
        axis.title.y       = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold", vjust = 1.8),
        legend.text        = element_text(size = 20),
        plot.caption       = element_text(face = "italic", size = 12, colour = "gray10"))

Injurytools package

df_summary        <- calc_summary(injd) 
## Warning in summary_unit(unit, out, quiet): 
##  Exposure time unit is matches_minutes
##  Case incidence and case burden are calculated per 100 athlete-matches of exposure (i.e. 90 minutes times 100)
## Warning in summary_unit(unit, out, quiet): 
##  Exposure time unit is matches_minutes
##  Case incidence and case burden are calculated per 100 athlete-matches of exposure (i.e. 90 minutes times 100)
df_summary_perinj <- calc_summary(injd, by = "injury_type") 
## Warning in summary_unit(unit, out, quiet): 
##  Exposure time unit is matches_minutes
##  Case incidence and case burden are calculated per 100 athlete-matches of exposure (i.e. 90 minutes times 100)
## Warning in summary_unit(unit, out, quiet): 
##  Exposure time unit is matches_minutes
##  Case incidence and case burden are calculated per 100 athlete-matches of exposure (i.e. 90 minutes times 100)
df_summary |> 
  mutate(incidence_new = paste0(round(incidence, 2), " (", round(incidence_lower, 2), ",", round(incidence_upper, 2), ")"),
         burden_new    = paste0(round(burden, 2), " (", round(burden_lower, 2), ",", round(burden_upper, 2), ")")) |> 
  dplyr::select(2, 7, 1, incidence_new, burden_new) |> 
  kable(col.names = c("N injuries", "N days lost", "Total expo", "Incidence (95% CI)", "Burden (95% CI)"),
        caption   = "Injury incidence and injury burden are reported as 100 player-matches",
        align     = "c")
Injury incidence and injury burden are reported as 100 player-matches
N injuries N days lost Total expo Incidence (95% CI) Burden (95% CI)
41 2579 64990 5.68 (3.94,7.42) 357.15 (343.36,370.93) 
df_summary_perinj |> 
  mutate(incidence_new = paste0(round(incidence, 2), " (", round(incidence_lower, 2), ",", round(incidence_upper, 2), ")"),
         burden_new    = paste0(round(burden, 2), " (", round(burden_lower, 2), ",", round(burden_upper, 2), ")")) |> 
  dplyr::select(1:2, 9, 4, incidence_new, burden_new) |> 
  kable(col.names = c("Type of injury", "N injuries", "N days lost", "Total expo", "Incidence (95% CI)", "Burden (95% CI)"),
        caption   = "Injury incidence and injury burden are reported as 100 player-matches",
        align     = "c")

Injurytools package

# warnings set to FALSE
palette <- c("#000000", Ligament = "#E69F00", Muscle = "#56B4E9", "#009E73",
             Unknown = "#F0E442", "#0072B2", Bone = "#D55E00", Concussion = "#CC79A7")
# source of the palette: http://www.cookbook-r.com/Graphs/Colors_(ggplot2)/
theme3 <- theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 20),
                axis.text.x.bottom = element_text(size = 20),
                axis.text.y.left = element_text(size = 20),
                axis.title.x = element_text(size = 15),
                axis.title.y = element_text(size = 15),
                legend.title = element_text(size = 15),
                legend.text = element_text(size = 15))

gg_riskmatrix(injd, 
              by = "injury_type", 
              title = "Risk matrix") +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Type of injury",
                    values = palette) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 5))) +
  theme3
## Warning in gg_riskmatrix(injd, by = "injury_type", title = "Risk matrix"): An injury average time-loss is bigger than its 75% quartile
##     time-loss. Possibly, there is a concrete long-term injury in the data.
##     For plotting purposes the vertical upper limit (i.e. 75% quartile)
##     is lengthened to the average value.
## Warning in geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = .data$incidence_lower, xmax =
## .data$incidence_upper), : Ignoring unknown parameters: `height`
## Warning in geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = .data$qt25_dayslost, ymax =
## .data$qt75_dayslost), : Ignoring unknown parameters: `height`

Injurytools package

theme4 <- theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 20),
                axis.text.x = element_text(size = 13.5),
                axis.text.y = element_text(size = 18),
                legend.title = element_text(size = 20),
                legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
                strip.text = element_text(size = 20))


gg_prevalence(injd, time_period = "monthly",
              line_mean = TRUE,
              title = "Monthly prevalence of sports injuries") + 
  theme4
